channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's transportation department cancels new review step that could've slowed state projects
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is backing off its demand for an extra layer of federal scrutiny whenever states seek even minor changes to their transportation plans, after the rescinded requirement sparked concern that some payments for roads, bridges and transit would be de
item.dc:creator: JEFF McMURRAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: I-40 to reopen at Tennessee-North Carolina state line
item.description: The four-mile stretch of I-40 on the Tennessee and North Carolina border will reopen to motor traffic Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Hillmer, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers will get a new email demanding their accomplishments, with a key change
item.description: Federal employees should expect another email on Saturday requiring them to explain their recent accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Hollywood icon Gene Hackman's and his wife's deaths
item.description: Hollywood icon Gene Hackman, his wife and one of their dogs were found dead in their New Mexico home, and investigators believe they had been dead for some time.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M boat with crew on board
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The number of people with measles in Texas increased to 146 in an outbreak that led this week to the death of an unvaccinated school-aged child, health officials said Friday. The number of cases — Texas’ largest in nearly 30 years — increased by 22 since Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A stretch of a North Carolina highway that collapsed during Helene is about to reopen
item.description: A stretch of Interstate 40 through the western North Carolina mountains is reopening to traffic this weekend, months after Hurricane Helene's historic flooding collapsed portions of the road, partially restoring the major travel connection with eastern Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vice President JD Vance, a Catholic, acknowledges the pope's criticism of US immigration crackdown
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged Pope Francis’ criticism of the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigration, without responding to any of its specifics or to the pontiff’s apparent criticism of Vance’s own deployment of Catholic tradition to justify such policies.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to sign order Friday designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign an executive order on Friday designating English as the official language of the United States, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to sign order to make English official language of US
item.description: President Trump is expected to sign an executive order to make English the official language of the United States, rescinding policies that require federal agencies to provide language assistance for non-English speakers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Midwest winters are changing. So is the ancient sport of falconry
item.description: GREENLEAF, Wis. (AP) — Stephanie Stevens has a good reason to love the bone-numbing cold of a Wisconsin winter. Every weekend, she loads up her minivan with a large green box and drives out to rural areas, usually the edges of friends' farm fields.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Most Americans who experienced severe winter weather see climate change at work, AP-NORC poll shows
item.description: A new poll finds that most U.S. adults who say severe cold was among the types of extreme weather they experienced in recent years cite climate change as a cause.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Georgia deputy who fatally shot exonerated man during traffic stop won't be charged
item.description: District Attorney Keith Higgins announced, following the GBI's findings, they will not seek criminal charges against a Georgia sheriff’s deputy involved in a 2023 fatal shooting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cardinal McElroy calls for compassion in immigration policy as he prepares to lead in Washington
item.description: Cardinal Robert McElroy, who is getting ready to take over as archbishop of Washington, D.C. in March, called for compassion and dignity for migrants Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No new bombshells in Justice Department’s release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday released government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, but the first wave of files posted on the Justice Department website have largely been circulating in the public domain for years and didn't include any new bombshells.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Map: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found as death toll climbs
item.description: An Indiana-based dairy company has recalled potentially affected shakes that were distributed nationwide to long-term care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Woman suspected in Colorado Tesla dealership vandalism charged in federal court
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Federal prosecutors have charged a woman in a string of vandalism against a Colorado Tesla dealership, including throwing Molotov cocktails at vehicles and spray painting “Nazi cars” on the building along with a message that appeared directed at company co-founder Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WGN 9

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein contact list: AG Pam Bondi releases highly anticipated DOJ documents
item.description: Many Jeffrey Epstein files released by the Justice Department Thursday had been made public in Ghislaine Maxwell's federal sex trafficking trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to save your video: Facebook to automatically delete live videos after 30 days
item.description: Facebook is changing its live video feature, allowing users to keep their videos for 30 days after publishing, after which they will be automatically deleted from Meta servers.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Thompson, KRON4

item.title: Betsy Arakawa, concert pianist married to actor Gene Hackman, dead at 65
item.description: Betsy Arakawa, a concert pianist and co-founder of a home furnishing business, was found dead Wednesday in her Santa Fe, New Mexico, home along with actor-husband Gene Hackman and their dog, according to authorities. She was 65.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 700 National Park Service employees are taking buyouts: reports
item.description: Earlier this month, the Trump administration fired about 1,000 newly hired National Park Service employees.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Gene Hackman was more than an everyman: An Appreciation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — One of the greatest American actors of the 20th century was voted “least likely to succeed” by his first theater school, wasn’t a star until he was 40 and possessed a face he once described as “your everyday mineworker.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Homicide fugitive from Washington state arrested in Utah by FBI task force
item.description: A 20-year-old homicide fugitive was arrested in Utah for the January death of a high school student in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg, 'Gossip Girl' and 'Buffy' actress, dead at 39
item.description: Actress Michelle Trachtenberg has died, police sources confirmed to multiple news outlets on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Diana Taurasi of the Phoenix Mercury retires after 20 WNBA seasons, 3 titles and 6 Olympic golds
item.description: Diana Taurasi is retiring after 20 seasons, ending one of the greatest careers in women's basketball history.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL and DOUG FEINBERG AP Basketball Writers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of himself
item.description: The video also features children holding gold balloons also made to look like Trump and a renovated beachfront with luxury hotels and money flying in the air.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
